target.title: test(codex): cover thread abandonment after completion-idle timeout
target.body:
## Summary

Regression coverage for #89974 ("Codex app-server turn idle timeout is surfaced as user interruption"). **No runtime behavior changes.**

- Confirms that after a `turn_completion_idle_timeout`, OpenClaw clears the timed-out Codex app-server thread binding.
- Confirms the next turn starts a fresh thread (`thread/start`) instead of resuming the thread that may still contain Codex's generic `<turn_aborted>` / user-interrupted marker.

The "user interrupted the previous turn on purpose" wording is Codex's own `<turn_aborted>` rollout marker; OpenClaw can't change it (`turn/interrupt` carries no reason), only avoid replaying it. The behavior that abandons the timed-out thread landed in #87781; this test pins it for the exact issue shape.

This verifies the fix at the **code/test level**; no live end-to-end repro was run.

## Validation

- new regression test: **passed**
- `run-attempt.turn-watches.test.ts`: **56 passed**
- `run.codex-app-server-recovery.test.ts`: **10 passed**
- oxfmt / oxlint / tsgo / `git diff --check`: **passed**

Refs #89974. Behavior fixed by #87781.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
